message:
test(fcp-core): pin Provenance::new(zone) canonical CBOR (saa7i)

Add crates/fcp-core/tests/provenance_cbor_golden.rs pinning the
canonical CBOR encoding of Provenance::new(zone) for the canonical
zone set: z:owner, z:private, z:work, z:project:demo, z:community,
z:public. The encoding feeds into content addressing and signing
transcripts; any drift would silently split content-address spaces
between revisions, so the regression must be visible in review.

Field-order note documented in the test: to_canonical_cbor sorts map
keys by canonical CBOR encoding bytes (RFC 8949 §4.2.1, length-then-
bytes), giving chain (5), taint (5), elevated (8), origin_zone (11)
in that order — NOT alphabetical. taint serializes as the string
"Untainted" (Serialize uses the variant name).

Pinned encoding is built from a shared PROVENANCE_PREFIX_HEX constant
(zone-independent header) plus the zone-id text-string suffix. Per
zone the test verifies: byte-stable encode, pinned hex, structural
decode equality (origin_zone, taint=Untainted, elevated=false, empty
chain, no elevation_token), and encode∘decode∘encode idempotence.
Two cross-cutting tests verify the prefix is zone-independent and
the six encodings are pairwise distinct.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
